title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
summary: A unified linear framework that addresses cross-modal alignment vs prediction. Under a spiked signal-plus-noise model, we derive separation ratios exposing complementary failure modes. The phase diagram partitions multimodal problems into four regimes: Both, CA only, CP only, and Neither.

title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
summary: EEVEE is the first multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents, enabling test-time prompt learning under real-world task streams. Introduces a router that partitions incoming inputs into task clusters. Improves average multi-benchmark scores by 10-24 points over Qwen3-4B and DeepSeek-V3.2.

title: Lip Forcing: Few-Step Autoregressive Diffusion for Real-time Lip Synchronization
summary: First autoregressive diffusion method for video-to-video lip synchronization. Distills a 14B teacher into causal students generating each chunk in only two denoising steps. The 1.3B student achieves 31 FPS; the 14B student runs 39.8x faster than its teacher at comparable fidelity.

title: Multi-Faceted Interactivity Alignment in Full-Duplex Speech Models
summary: Proposes a post-training alignment method that comprehensively improves interactivity of full-duplex spoken dialogue models through RL. Addresses four canonical axes: pause handling, turn-taking, backchanneling, and user interruption.

title: TRACE: A Unified Rollout Budget Allocation Framework for Efficient Agentic Reinforcement Learning
summary: Models each ReAct-style thought-action-observation turn as a semantically distinct node, allowing budget allocation to extend from prompt roots to turn-level prefixes with further continuations, forming tree-structured rollouts.
